reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21 — Basic Pitch runnability + correctness proven FIRST in headless Chrome (pure 440 Hz -> MIDI 69, 261.6 Hz -> MIDI 60; synthesized chord/arpeggio/duet clips transcribe to intended pitches with 4-5 simultaneous keys), then the deployed page driven end-to-end (chords auto-transcribed to a piano roll + picked notes, arpeggio re-transcribes). Sample clips are self-authored (synthesized in scripts/gen-basic-pitch-samples.mjs) — no third-party audio.

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY + CORRECTNESS verified in headless Chrome (onnxruntime-web 1.21.0, WASM EP, no GPU) BEFORE building. A pure 440 Hz tone peaks at MIDI 69 (A4, 0.50) and 261.6 Hz at MIDI 60 (C4, 0.56) — exact pitch mapping. The three self-authored piano-like clips transcribe to their intended notes with up to 4-5 simultaneous keys active (genuine POLYPHONY). Deployed page (scripts/validate-basic-pitch.mjs 9/9): the chords sample auto-transcribed to 7 note events (C4,E4,G4,F4,C5) with a piano roll of 20189 lit pixels, ~37 ms/window. The piano roll IS the model's raw note head — nothing canned.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a module Worker. Model is ~0.3 MB and ~37-60 ms per ~2 s window, so a several-second clip transcribes near-instantly.

rubric.evidence: Raw mono audio [1,43844,1] f32 at 22050 Hz per ~1.99 s window (harmonic-CQT frontend is inside the graph) -> note [1,172,88] + onset [1,172,88] (MIDI 21-108) + contour [1,172,264] (3 bins/semitone). A DISTINCT audio capability: POLYPHONIC music transcription / audio-to-MIDI, vs the built monophonic pitch-detection (CREPE) which tracks a single fundamental.
rubric.notes: Arbitrary-length audio is processed in consecutive 43844-sample windows and the frame outputs concatenated; frame time = 256/22050 s (~11.6 ms). Note vs onset head disambiguated by the model's output order (validated: the first 88-wide head is the sustained note, the second the sparse onset).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the model (Spotify Basic Pitch, linked to github.com/spotify/basic-pitch), task (music-transcription / audio-to-MIDI), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21 — noting transformers.js has no such task), the real I/O shapes per window, quantisation (fp32), backend (WASM), download (~0.3 MB) and license (Apache-2.0, Spotify). No invented API.
rubric.notes: onnxruntime-web is pinned in THIS worker only (isolated per-worker escape hatch, like raft/reid/face-iqa); shared libs untouched.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'spotify/basic-pitch', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:0.3}). Weights are fetched THROUGH Cache Storage under a key carrying the model-id path (huggingface.co/spotify/basic-pitch/resolve/main/nmp.onnx) so lib/model-cache.js scans '/spotify/basic-pitch' -> a returning visit auto-initialises with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download (~0.3 MB). Verified: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it flipped to ready and auto-transcribed the chords sample.
rubric.notes: Tiny model so re-download is cheap, but the never-silently-download guarantee + auto-init on return still hold via the shared loader; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (absent) -> ready (auto-transcribes the chords sample: piano roll + note list + status) -> re-transcribe on any sample/upload/mic; a busy guard prevents overlapping runs; error path via the shared loader + a per-run try/catch that surfaces the message in the status line. Verified download -> ready -> chords -> arpeggio sequence end-to-end.
rubric.notes: Mic path caps at ~5 s and stops the tracks on end. Very low bass notes (below ~C3) are harder for the model on synthetic tones — an inherent model characteristic, noted honestly.

rubric.evidence: Three self-authored sample chips (chords / arpeggio / two-hand duet), a drag-and-drop + click file upload (accept=audio/*), and a mic record button (toggle, auto-stop at 5 s). Each path decodes to 22050 Hz mono and transcribes. All chips are native buttons with inline SVG icons (no emoji).
rubric.notes: Covers the three natural inputs — canned demo audio, the user's own file, and live capture.

rubric.evidence: The piano roll is the model's raw note-activation matrix — every lit cell is a pitch the network heard at that frame. The note-event list is a SIMPLE peak-pick over the note + onset heads, and the UI says so explicitly ('not Basic Pitch's full note-creation algorithm'). Sample clips are synthesized in-repo (scripts/gen-basic-pitch-samples.mjs), not third-party recordings, and the footer links the generator.
rubric.notes: Honest about limits: the picked note list is a simplified extraction, and harmonic ghosting / weak low bass can appear — real characteristics of running a model trained on real instruments over synthetic tones.

rubric.evidence: Status is role=status aria-live=polite; the piano-roll canvas has role=img + aria-label; the note list is aria-live; the dropzone is a focusable role=button with Enter/Space activation and a visible focus outline; sample/mic controls are native buttons with text labels (SVG icons are aria-hidden).
rubric.notes: The piano roll is inherently visual; the picked-note list gives a non-visual read of the same result (note names + timing in titles).

rubric.evidence: Controls are flex-wrap; the piano-roll canvas is width:100% inside an overflow-x:auto wrapper; the note list wraps and scrolls. Verified 0 horizontal overflow at desktop AND mobile 360px (validate-basic-pitch.mjs), 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Index verified desktop+mobile; a subpage ladder is a recorded follow-up.

rubric.evidence: All inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; ~37-60 ms per ~2 s window on single-thread WASM. A several-second clip transcribes in well under a second; the ~0.3 MB download is negligible. Note/onset matrices are transferred (not copied) back to the main thread.
rubric.notes: The canvas piano roll is drawn once per transcription; no per-frame animation loop, so it stays cheap.

guidanceConsulted.query: run an ONNX audio model off the main thread in a web worker; decode + resample audio to a fixed sample rate in the browser
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move inference off the main thread; keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid blocking the UI on model work.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — transcription runs in a module Worker; audio is decoded + resampled to 22050 Hz via OfflineAudioContext, matrices transferred back; a busy guard avoids overlapping runs.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: ~37 ms/window; page stayed responsive; harness saw no long-task console errors

guidanceConsulted.query: wide canvas visualization that scrolls horizontally without making the page body overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Contain wide content (canvas/tables) in an overflow-x:auto wrapper; use width:100%/max-width:100% so the body never scrolls sideways.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the piano-roll canvas is width:100% inside a .bp-roll-wrap { overflow-x:auto }; controls + note list flex-wrap.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible file dropzone + live-updating result region announced to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Make custom drop targets focusable role=button with keyboard activation; announce changing results via aria-live; label canvas content.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — dropzone is role=button tabindex=0 with Enter/Space + focus outline; status + note list are aria-live; the canvas has role=img + aria-label.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: keyboard opens the file picker; status announces the note-event count and timing

openQuestions: Is a faithful port of Basic Pitch's output_to_notes_polyphonic (onset gating + energy tracking + min-note-length) worth the code, or is the raw piano roll + simple peak-pick the more honest showcase?
How well does the fixed non-overlapping windowing hold up on long real recordings vs Basic Pitch's overlap-and-unwrap — are seam artifacts audible in the piano roll?
followUpGoals: Add a faithful note-creation pass + MIDI-file export ('download .mid') so the audio-to-MIDI story is end-to-end.
Use overlapping windows with edge-frame trimming (Basic Pitch's unwrap) so long uploads have no seam artifacts.
Build a basics/practical/wild subpage ladder (single-note sanity, real polyphonic recording, dense/percussive audio where transcription is hard).
summary: Music transcription (Spotify Basic Pitch) — a genuinely new audio family (polyphonic audio-to-MIDI, distinct from the monophonic CREPE pitch-detection), proven to run + map pitches correctly in the browser on ORT-web WASM (440 Hz -> A4, 261.6 Hz -> C4; chords/duet show 4-5 simultaneous keys). Tiny (~0.3 MB), ~37 ms/window. Piano roll is the raw model output; note list is a labelled simple peak-pick. Index demo complete + verified 9/9; full note-creation + MIDI export + subpage ladder are recorded follow-ups.
